toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

data.reasoningText:
This is a .NET 9.0 MSTest project. The user wants to:
1. Run the tests
2. Collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process

For .NET, to collect crash dumps on crash, we can set environment variables:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — full dump (MiniDumpWithFullMemory). Values: 1=Mini, 2=Heap, 3=Triage, 4=Full


- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=<path>` — where to write the dump

Alternatively, I can use `dotnet test --blame-crash`, which is the built-in option specifically for capturing crash dumps when the test host process crashes. This is simpler than manually setting environment variables, so I'll go with that approach.

arguments.description: Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-4605c9c17fd5464aaef58c876d37fabc && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with --blame-crash to collect crash dumps", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's what was run:

```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

The `--blame-crash` flag enables the blame data collector, which will automatically capture a full crash dump if the test host process crashes. Since no crash occurred, no dump was generated. If a future test causes a crash, the dump will be written to the `TestResults/` directory alongside the test results.
